name: betterstack
description: Read Better Stack Uptime monitors, incidents, and heartbeats

envVars.name: BETTERSTACK_API_TOKEN
envVars.description: Better Stack Uptime API token (global token or team-scoped Uptime token)

tools.name: List Monitors
tools.description: List uptime monitors, optionally filtered by URL or name

pronounceable_name.description: Filter monitors by their pronounceable name

historicalSummary.omitted: regional check configuration and notification settings

tools.name: Get Monitor
tools.description: Get a monitor's configuration and current status

tools.name: Get Monitor Availability
tools.description: Get a monitor's availability (SLA) summary for a time range

tools.name: List Incidents
tools.description: List incidents, optionally limited to a date range or unresolved only

from.description: Only incidents starting on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

to.description: Only incidents starting on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

resolved.description: Set false to list only active (unresolved) incidents

tools.name: List Heartbeats
tools.description: List heartbeat (cron/job) monitors and their status

prompts.title: What's down?
prompts.prompt: List my Better Stack monitors and flag any that are not in the 'up' status, plus any unresolved incidents.

prompts.title: Uptime report
prompts.prompt: Get the availability summary for my main Better Stack monitor over the last 30 days.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Better Stack Uptime API Setup

steps.title: Sign up for Better Stack
steps.description: Create a free account at https://betterstack.com and add at least one uptime monitor or heartbeat so the API returns data.

steps.title: Create an API token
steps.description: In Better Stack go to API tokens. Use a global API token, or create a team-scoped token in the 'Uptime API tokens' section.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Add the token to your .env as BETTERSTACK_API_TOKEN=...

notes: This connector covers the Uptime API (uptime.betterstack.com) only — the Telemetry/Logs API uses a different token and host and is not included
Responses follow JSON:API: items are under data[] with attributes, pagination links under pagination
Incidents use the newer /api/v3 path; monitors and heartbeats use /api/v2
